does: This paper proposes white-box jailbreak attacks against large vision-language models by jointly optimizing adversarial perturbations across both image and text modalities. The method starts by optimizing an adversarial image prefix from random noise to generate diverse harmful responses, then co-optimizes an adversarial text suffix alongside the image prefix using dual optimization objectives that guide the model toward affirmative responses with high toxicity. The approach exploits vulnerabilities across multimodal inputs rather than treating modalities independently, demonstrating that VLMs can be compromised through coordinated attacks that combine visual and linguistic adversarial perturbations to elicit responses to harmful instructions.
connects: This work builds on the growing literature examining adversarial robustness in vision-language models, extending beyond prior research that primarily focused on unimodal attacks either through image perturbations or independent text manipulations. It responds to the gap in understanding how VLMs handle coordinated multimodal attacks, drawing on foundational adversarial attack methodology and optimization techniques from both computer vision and NLP domains. The dual optimization framework integrates insights from image-based adversarial attack strategies with text-based jailbreak techniques, addressing the implicit assumption in earlier work that text-based attacks posed less risk than visual perturbations in multimodal systems.
enables: This research establishes a more comprehensive threat model for VLM safety evaluation and robustness assessment, pointing toward the need for multimodal defense mechanisms that address coordinated attacks rather than isolated modality vulnerabilities. The findings suggest future work developing defenses that operate jointly across text and image modalities, improved adversarial training procedures for VLMs, and more sophisticated evaluation benchmarks that measure resilience to coordinated multimodal perturbations. Additionally, it enables better understanding of how different modalities interact in VLM decision-making and could motivate development of alignment techniques specifically designed to address multimodal input vulnerabilities in large-scale vision-language systems.
